title: The coldest place in the universe isn't empty space #Shorts
desc: The coldest known place in the whole universe isn't the emptiness between the stars. It's the Boomerang Nebula, about 5,000 light-years away, sitting near one degree above absolute zero (roughly minus 458 Fahrenheit) — actually colder than the leftover heat of the Big Bang that fills all of space. A dying star is flinging out gas so fast that the rapid expansion chills it below deep space itself, like a giant cosmic refrigerator.
